title: FC Dacia Pitești

infobox.value: (IPA: [[ˈdatʃi.a piˈteʃtʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")), was a Romanian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [club](/wiki/Club_(organization) "Club (organization)") based in [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"), [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"). Dacia was founded in 1953 under the name of *Metalul Colibași*, then since 1969 bearing the name of the famous factory [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia"), owner and main sponsor of the club. The club was dissolved in 2001, as a result of a merge with [AS Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni"), merge by which Dacia was absorbed.

+ **Winners (2):** [1968–69](/w/index.php?title=1968%E2%80%9369_Divizia_D&action=edit&redlink=1 "1968–69 Divizia D (page does not exist)"), [1975–76](/w/index.php?title=1975%E2%80%9376_Divizia_D&action=edit&redlink=1 "1975–76 Divizia D (page does not exist)")
* Best finish in [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"): **4th** ([1999–2000](/wiki/1999%E2%80%932000_Divizia_B "1999–2000 Divizia B"))

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Romanian football club

Not to be confused with [CS Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni").

Football club

Dacia Pitești

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Full name | Fotbal Club Dacia Pitești |
| Nickname | * *Echipa uzinei* (Factory's Team) |
| Short name | Dacia |
| Founded | 1953 as *Metalul Colibași* |
| Dissolved | 2001 *absorbed by [Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni")* |
| Ground | [Dacia](/w/index.php?title=Stadionul_Dacia_(%C8%98tef%C4%83ne%C8%99ti)&action=edit&redlink=1 "Stadionul Dacia (Ștefănești) (page does not exist)") |
| Capacity | 1,000 |
| Owner | [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia") |
| [2000–01](/wiki/2000%E2%80%9301_Divizia_C "2000–01 Divizia C") | Divizia C, Seria V, 6th |

**Fotbal Club Dacia Pitești**, commonly known as **Dacia Pitești** (IPA: [[ˈdatʃi.a piˈteʃtʲ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Romanian "Help:IPA/Romanian")), was a Romanian [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") [club](/wiki/Club_(organization) "Club (organization)") based in [Pitești](/wiki/Pite%C8%99ti "Pitești"), [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"). Dacia was founded in 1953 under the name of *Metalul Colibași*, then since 1969 bearing the name of the famous factory [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia"), owner and main sponsor of the club. The club was dissolved in 2001, as a result of a merge with [AS Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni"), merge by which Dacia was absorbed.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

**Dacia Pitești** was founded in 1953 in [Mioveni](/wiki/Mioveni "Mioveni"), [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"), under the name of *Metalul Colibași* (Mioveni was named at that time as Colibași).[[1]](#cite_note-1) Metalul promoted for the first time in the national divisions at the end of the 1968–69, after winning [Argeș County Championship](/wiki/Liga_IV_Arge%C8%99 "Liga IV Argeș") and the promotion play-off against Vagonul Ploiești, the champion of [Prahova County](/wiki/Liga_IV_Prahova "Liga IV Prahova") (2–1 at Colibași and 0–0 at Ploiești). After promotion, the club entered under the ownership of [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia") and changed its name to **Dacia Pitești**, (sometimes known also as *Dacia Colibași*).[[2]](#cite_note-2) Dacia spent six seasons at the level of [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"), with a 3rd place, as its best performance, at the end of the [1972–73](/wiki/1972%E2%80%9373_Divizia_C "1972–73 Divizia C") season. The boys from the factory relegated at the end of the [1974–75](/wiki/1974%E2%80%9375_Divizia_C "1974–75 Divizia C") season, a very weak one for them, ranking 15th of 16.[[3]](#cite_note-Liga_3-3)

Dacia promoted back after only one season spent in the county leagues, then followed a long run in the third tier, no less than 12 years, a period in which the club was ranked mostly in the middle of the table, with two exceptions: 2nd place at the end of the [1982–83](/wiki/1982%E2%80%9383_Divizia_C "1982–83 Divizia C") season (10 years later from their previous best performance) and 1st place at the end of the [1987–88](/wiki/1987%E2%80%9388_Divizia_C "1987–88 Divizia C") season.[[3]](#cite_note-Liga_3-3) After this last performance, Dacia promoted for the first time in the [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II").[[4]](#cite_note-Liga_2-4)

It was a short adventure for the squad of [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia"), as it would be relegated at the end of the season, after being ranked as 16th of 18, only one point away from the first safe place, occupied by [AS Drobeta-Turnu Severin](/wiki/FC_Drobeta-Turnu_Severin "FC Drobeta-Turnu Severin"). The boys from [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County") won Seria VII of Divizia C at the end of 1991–92 season, but missed the promotion after an unsuccessful play-off. Dacia finally promoted back to Divizia B in the summer of 1994 (after an absence of 5 years), as it was crowned champion of Seria III of Divizia C, 11 points over the runner-up, Petrolul Stoina.[[3]](#cite_note-Liga_3-3)

The following six years (between 1994 and 2000) it is considered as the best period in the history of the club, Dacia played constantly (with good results) at the level of [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"). In this six years, the squad was ranked as it follows: 6th ([1994–95](/wiki/1994%E2%80%9395_Divizia_B "1994–95 Divizia B")), 7th ([1995–96](/wiki/1995%E2%80%9396_Divizia_B "1995–96 Divizia B")), 5th ([1996–97](/wiki/1996%E2%80%9397_Divizia_B "1996–97 Divizia B")), 10th ([1997–98](/wiki/1997%E2%80%9398_Divizia_B "1997–98 Divizia B")), 13th ([1998–99](/wiki/1998%E2%80%9399_Divizia_B "1998–99 Divizia B")) and 4th ([1999–2000](/wiki/1999%E2%80%932000_Divizia_B "1999–2000 Divizia B")) – the best result in the history of the club.[[4]](#cite_note-Liga_2-4) Also in this period, the club was considered as a feeder club for [FC Argeș Pitești](/wiki/FC_Arge%C8%99_Pite%C8%99ti "FC Argeș Pitești").

Ironically, after Dacia obtained the best ranking in its history, the club decided to sell its [Divizia B](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II") place to [Politehnica Timișoara](/wiki/FC_Politehnica_Timi%C8%99oara "FC Politehnica Timișoara"), a club that relegated to the third tier in that season. As a result, Dacia took Politehnica's place in the third tier and also gave up a few players to Timișoara, among them [Alin Chița](/wiki/Alin_Chi%C8%9Ba "Alin Chița"), [Cristian Bratu](/wiki/Cristian_Bratu "Cristian Bratu"), [Marius Diță](/wiki/Marius_Di%C8%9B%C4%83 "Marius Diță") and Daniel Stanciu.[[5]](#cite_note-5) Following these changes, Dacia played for another season at the level of [Divizia C](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III") (ranked 6th of 15), then in the summer of 2001 the club merged with [AS Mioveni](/wiki/CS_Mioveni "CS Mioveni"). As a result of this merge, Dacia Pitești was absorbed in AS Mioveni, club that was renamed as *AS Dacia Mioveni* and started to be sponsored by [Automobile Dacia](/wiki/Automobile_Dacia "Automobile Dacia").

## Ground

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ground")]

**Dacia Pitești** played its home matches on Stadionul Colibași in [Mioveni](/wiki/Mioveni "Mioveni") or on Stadionul Dacia in [Ștefănești](/wiki/%C8%98tef%C4%83ne%C8%99ti,_Arge%C8%99 "Ștefănești, Argeș"), both situated in [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County") and with a capacity of approx. 1,000 seats.

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Honours")]

* **[Liga III](/wiki/Liga_III "Liga III"):**
  + **Winners (3):** [1987–88](/wiki/1987%E2%80%9388_Divizia_C "1987–88 Divizia C"), [1991–92](/wiki/1991%E2%80%9392_Divizia_C "1991–92 Divizia C"), [1993–94](/wiki/1993%E2%80%9394_Divizia_C "1993–94 Divizia C")
  + Runners-up (1): [1982–83](/wiki/1982%E2%80%9383_Divizia_C "1982–83 Divizia C")
* **[Liga IV](/wiki/Liga_IV "Liga IV") – [Argeș County](/wiki/Arge%C8%99_County "Argeș County"):**
  + **Winners (2):** [1968–69](/w/index.php?title=1968%E2%80%9369_Divizia_D&action=edit&redlink=1 "1968–69 Divizia D (page does not exist)"), [1975–76](/w/index.php?title=1975%E2%80%9376_Divizia_D&action=edit&redlink=1 "1975–76 Divizia D (page does not exist)")

### Other performances

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=FC_Dacia_Pite%C8%99ti&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Other performances")]

* Appearances in [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"): **7**
* Best finish in [Liga II](/wiki/Liga_II "Liga II"): **4th** ([1999–2000](/wiki/1999%E2%80%932000_Divizia_B "1999–2000 Divizia B"))
* Best finish in [Cupa României](/wiki/Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "Cupa României"): **Round of 32** ([1975–76](/wiki/1975%E2%80%9376_Cupa_Rom%C3%A2niei "1975–76 Cupa României"))
